registry.description: Rebellion is the wilful rejection of legitimate authority — the active refusal to be governed by one who has the right to govern. The Hebrew vocabulary is vivid: revolt, transgression, the active pushing against constraint. Rebellion against God is treated as the core of sin in the prophetic literature: Israel's history is narrated as a long pattern of departing from God and returning. Rebellion is not confusion or weakness but deliberate opposition. It is the posture of the person who knows the command and refuses it.

terms.medium_def:
: turn aside/deflect
1) to turn aside, depart
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to turn aside, turn in unto
1a2) to depart, depart from way, avoid
1a3) to be removed
1a4) to come to an end
1b) (Polel) to turn aside
1c) (Hiphil)
1c1) to cause to turn aside, cause to depart, remove, take away, put away, depose
1c2) to put aside, leave undone, retract, reject, abolish
1d) (Hophal) to be taken away, be removed
